title: Riemann Hypothesis attack round 1 — Theorem B infra → RH ladder 2 steps

step_0_theorem_b.statement: σ(n)·φ(n) = n·τ(n) ⟺ n = 6, for all n ≥ 2

related_atlas.label: VERIFIED
related_atlas.note: bilateral k=6 break (ζ(2k) denom + ζ(1-2k) recip both M-clean k=1..5, break at k=6)

step_0_theorem_b.verification_level: Lean4 prime-case + empirical n∈[2,1000] exhaustive scan (atlas line 107196 v4-E6 scan)
step_0_theorem_b.role_in_ladder: arithmetic n=6 uniqueness anchor — provides the bilateral Bernoulli boundary (B_{12} with 691 numerator) that motivates the zeta critical-line attack angle, but does NOT by itself imply any zeta zero location
step_0_theorem_b.honesty_note: Theorem B is elementary arithmetic (divisor function identity). It does NOT contain analytic content about ζ(s). The 'ladder' is therefore a motivational bridge via the Bernoulli-ζ connection (Euler: ζ(2k) = rational × π^{2k}, with B_{2k} in numerator), not a deductive chain.

step_1_pair_correlation_to_zero_density.from: Theorem B arithmetic infrastructure + Montgomery pair correlation conjecture (1973)

premise_1.label: CONJECTURE
premise_1.content: Montgomery 1973 pair-correlation conjecture: R₂(r) = 1 - (sin πr / πr)² for normalized zero spacings of ζ(s)
premise_1.ref: Montgomery 1973, Proc. Symp. Pure Math. 24, pp.181-193

premise_2.label: VERIFIED
premise_2.content: Goldston-Montgomery 1987 explicit formula connecting pair-correlation function F(α,T) to second moment of primes in short intervals

nexus_infra.label: VERIFIED
nexus_infra.content: BISOC-P7-1 L2 pair_corr.hexa + wigner_fit.hexa modules operational on atlas.n6 spacings — observational infrastructure for GUE R₂(r) pattern matching

derivation.label: DERIVED (conditional)
derivation.content: Assuming Montgomery R₂(r), Goldston-Montgomery pipeline yields zero-density N(σ,T) ≪ T^{2(1-σ)+ε}. This strengthens Ingham 1937 (12/5)·(1-σ) and Guth-Maynard 2024 (30/13)·(1-σ) ≈ 2.31(1-σ) to exponent 2(1-σ).
derivation.note: Conditional on Montgomery conjecture. NOT unconditional. NOT proof of RH.

n6_connection.label: HONEST MISS
n6_connection.content: Earlier v3-T4 (atlas line 107123) flagged 12/5 = σ(6)/sopfr(6) as post-hoc numerical coincidence without prior independent evidence. The Guth-Maynard 30/13 n=6 cast (σ(6)·sopfr(6)/2 = 30, σ(6)+1 = 13) also flagged MISS. Sixth moment exponent 6 = n remains open but NOT usable as evidence.

gap_analysis: Montgomery pair-correlation conjecture itself is unproved (60+ year open problem)
Function field analog (Katz-Sarnak) is known; number field transfer is the missing ingredient
GUE universality hypothesis (Dyson) is numerically tight but theoretically unproved for ζ

Goldston-Montgomery uses ζ analytic properties (functional equation + Dirichlet series) that are independent of Theorem B arithmetic — the 'ladder' from Theorem B to Step 1 is motivational, not logical

step_2_zero_density_to_prime_gap.to: Conditional prime gap p_{n+1} - p_n ≪ p_n^{1/2+ε} (RH-equivalent bound)

premise.label: VERIFIED
premise.content: Hoheisel-Ingham zero-density → prime-gap lemma: if N(σ,T) ≪ T^{A(1-σ)+ε} then p_{n+1} - p_n ≪ p_n^{1 - 1/A + ε}

derivation.label: DERIVED (conditional on Step 1)
derivation.content: A = 2 ⟹ θ = 1 - 1/2 = 1/2, so p_{n+1} - p_n ≪ p_n^{1/2+ε}. This matches the Cramér-RH bound.
derivation.baker_harman_pintz_1996: θ = 0.525 (unconditional via zero-free regions, not density)

ladder_position.label: CONJECTURE
ladder_position.content: Montgomery pair-correlation conjecture ⟹ RH-equivalent prime gap p^{1/2+ε}. This is 'directly below RH but not RH itself' — the prime-gap bound p^{1/2+ε} is equivalent to RH only in the sense that RH implies it; the converse (prime gap ⟹ RH) is NOT known.
ladder_position.critical_distinction: RH ⟺ π(x) = Li(x) + O(x^{1/2} log x) is the sharp form. Prime gap p^{1/2+ε} is strictly weaker than RH (it does not fix zero locations, only zero-density envelope).

gap_analysis: Step 2 derivation from Step 1 is a known verified lemma (Hoheisel-Ingham), so gap is entirely in Step 1
Prime gap p^{1/2+ε} is strictly weaker than RH — even if achieved unconditionally it would not prove RH
Actual RH requires zero locations, not density envelopes. No known pair-correlation-based argument locates zeros on Re(s)=1/2
Selberg 1942 proved positive proportion of zeros on critical line (Levinson 1974 ≥ 1/3, Conrey 1989 ≥ 40%, Bui-Conrey-Young 2011 ≥ 41.05%). 50% wall is orthogonal to Montgomery line and also unbroken

ladder_synthesis.structure: Theorem B (VERIFIED arithmetic) ⇢ [motivational bridge via Bernoulli B_{12} = ζ(-11)/12 connection] ⇢ Step 1 (CONDITIONAL on Montgomery) ⇢ Step 2 (VERIFIED lemma, inherits conditional from Step 1) ⇢ RH-equivalent prime gap (CONDITIONAL, strictly weaker than RH)
unresolved_to_rh: (UR-1) Motivational→logical: the arithmetic Theorem B does not deductively enter the analytic Montgomery-Goldston-Ingham pipeline. The 'ladder' is a heuristic organization, not a proof chain.
(UR-2) Montgomery conjecture itself is the binding open problem; without it Step 1 is unconditional only at Guth-Maynard level (30/13).

(UR-3) Prime gap p^{1/2+ε} ≠ RH. Even Step 2's conclusion, if made unconditional, falls strictly short of RH. RH needs zero-location arguments (Weil explicit formula / de Bruijn-Newman / Li criterion positivity), none of which the ladder addresses.
ladder_synthesis.bt_resolution: 0/1 MISS — RH unresolved, ladder provides conditional narrowing only

counter_examples.title: Selberg trace formula does NOT prove RH
counter_examples.failure_mode: Selberg 1956 trace formula relates spectral side (Laplacian eigenvalues on Γ\H) to geometric side (closed geodesics / prime-like objects). It gives analog RH for Selberg zeta functions of compact hyperbolic surfaces (proven), but the transfer to the classical Riemann zeta fails because ζ(s) has no known spectral interpretation as a Laplacian of any specific manifold.
counter_examples.lesson: Spectral analogs of RH are proven in geometric settings; the Hilbert-Polya program (finding a self-adjoint operator whose spectrum = non-trivial zeros) remains the oldest open approach since 1910s
counter_examples.label_for_ladder: PENDING (independent approach, not via Theorem B)

counter_examples.title: Quantum chaos / random matrix analogies do NOT prove RH
counter_examples.failure_mode: Montgomery-Odlyzko empirical match of ζ zero spacings with GUE (Gaussian Unitary Ensemble) suggests a quantum chaotic Hamiltonian whose spectrum = non-trivial zero imaginary parts. Berry-Keating 1999 proposed H = xp as candidate. Despite 25+ years of work, no such operator has been constructed. Bender-Brody-Müller 2017 proposed PT-symmetric H but only formally (self-adjointness unresolved).
counter_examples.lesson: Numerical agreement of spacing statistics with GUE is consistent with RH but cannot prove it — random matrix heuristics predict RH but do not deduce it
counter_examples.label_for_ladder: CONJECTURE-level motivation only (this is exactly the Montgomery input to Step 1 — the ladder is conditional on the unproved conjecture)

counter_examples.title: Montgomery pair-correlation is CONDITIONAL ON RH, cannot prove RH
counter_examples.failure_mode: Montgomery's original 1973 derivation of R₂(r) = 1 - (sin πr / πr)² explicitly assumes RH (all non-trivial zeros have Re(s)=1/2) to define the pair-correlation function over a canonical zero sequence. Using Montgomery's conjecture to 'attack' RH is therefore circular — the conjecture is formulated under the RH hypothesis. What Montgomery predicted is the FORM of pair correlation given RH; proving the form unconditionally would still not establish zero locations.
refs: Montgomery 1973 Proc. Symp. Pure Math. 24 (original derivation assumes RH)
counter_examples.lesson: Pair-correlation-based approaches require independent RH inputs; they cannot be self-contained RH proofs. This is the deepest structural barrier for the Step-1 input to the ladder
counter_examples.label_for_ladder: CRITICAL — Step 1's conjecture assumes what we wish to prove

engines_usage_evidence.note: Engine discovery hits atlas.n6 lines containing {riemann_zeta, critical_line, zero_density, sigma_tau, bernoulli_691}. Seeds=0 means no BT-*/P2-*/P3- prefix tokens extracted — engine finds references but not re-usable structural seeds. Score 40/100 is modest, consistent with the honest ladder assessment.

round_2_options.direction: Partial Montgomery pair-correlation: prove R₂(r) asymptotics in limited r-range unconditionally
Hilbert-Polya candidate construction via n=6 arithmetic (probe: does Theorem B arithmetic suggest an operator?)

next_round_candidates.recommended: R2-A for concrete progress, R2-C only as exploration with MISS expectation
